_metadata.description: Complete ontology of residential construction domain. Models entities and their relationships as a property graph. Designed for future RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) integration and semantic search via FAISS. Every entity is connected to related entities, enabling the AI to traverse the knowledge graph to answer domain questions.
_metadata.rag_notes: For FAISS integration, each entity should be embedded as: entity.name + ' ' + entity.description + ' ' + related_entity_names. Relationship types act as edge labels in the knowledge graph.

trades.name: General Labor
trades.description: Non-specialized workers who assist all trades with material handling, cleanup, and support tasks.

trades.name: Concrete Trade
trades.description: Specialists in concrete forming, pouring, and finishing. Includes concrete finishers and form carpenters.

trades.name: Framing Carpenter
trades.description: Structural framing specialists. Build all floor systems, wall assemblies, and roof framing.

trades.name: Electrician
trades.description: Licensed electrical contractors who install all electrical systems. Work in two phases: rough-in and finish.

trades.name: Plumber
trades.description: Licensed plumbing contractors who install drain-waste-vent systems and water supply. Work in rough-in and finish phases.

trades.name: HVAC Technician
trades.description: Heating, ventilation, and air conditioning specialists. Install ductwork, equipment, and commission systems.

trades.name: Roofer
trades.description: Roofing specialists who install underlayment, shingles, flashing, and roofing accessories.

trades.name: Drywall Installer
trades.description: Specialists who hang gypsum board panels and apply joint compound finish coats.

trades.name: Painter
trades.description: Apply primer and paint to interior and exterior surfaces.

trades.name: Finish Carpenter
trades.description: Install interior trim, doors, windows, and millwork with precision.

trades.name: Flooring Installer
trades.description: Install hardwood, LVP, laminate, and carpet flooring.

trades.name: Tile Setter
trades.description: Set ceramic and porcelain tile in bathrooms, kitchens, and other areas.

trades.name: Cabinet Installer
trades.description: Install kitchen and bathroom cabinets with precision leveling.

materials.name: Ready-Mix Concrete
materials.storage_conditions: Cannot be stored — delivered and placed immediately

materials.name: Dimensional Lumber 2x4
materials.storage_conditions: Keep dry — stain rapidly in rain

materials.name: LVL Beam (Laminated Veneer Lumber)
materials.lead_time_notes: Engineered lumber must be ordered 2-4 weeks in advance

materials.name: Roof Trusses
materials.lead_time_notes: Factory-fabricated custom component. Must order 3-6 weeks before needed.

safety_hazards.name: Falls from Height
safety_hazards.description: Falls are the leading cause of construction fatalities. Any work above 6 feet requires fall protection.

safety_hazards.name: Struck by Object
safety_hazards.description: Workers struck by vehicles, materials, or equipment. Common during all stages.

safety_hazards.name: Trench/Excavation Collapse
safety_hazards.description: Cave-in of excavation walls. Soil weighs 100 lbs per cubic foot. Instantaneous burial.
prevention_measures: slope or shore all excavations over 5 feet
keep equipment away from trench edge

safety_hazards.name: Electrocution
safety_hazards.description: Contact with energized conductors. Particularly dangerous during service entrance work.
prevention_measures: never work on energized conductors alone

safety_hazards.name: Concrete Chemical Burns
safety_hazards.description: Wet concrete pH 12-13 causes full-thickness chemical burns. Eye contact can cause blindness.

safety_hazards.name: Nail Gun Injury
safety_hazards.description: 37,000 nail gun injuries per year. Contact trip nailers can double-fire. 3.5" nails penetrate completely through hands.
prevention_measures: keep finger off trigger when moving

safety_hazards.name: Silica Dust Inhalation
safety_hazards.description: Respirable crystalline silica causes silicosis (incurable lung disease). Drywall sanding, concrete cutting, and tile cutting generate hazardous silica dust.

safety_hazards.name: Heat Stress and Heat Stroke
safety_hazards.description: Roof surfaces reach 150°F+ in summer. Heat stroke is a medical emergency. Roofers have highest heat-related illness rate.
prevention_measures: schedule work in early morning in summer

safety_hazards.name: Sheet Metal Lacerations
safety_hazards.description: Raw sheet metal edges are razor sharp. Most HVAC injuries are lacerations.
prevention_measures: heavy leather gloves when handling sheet metal
never grab raw sheet metal bare-handed

safety_hazards.name: VOC Inhalation
safety_hazards.description: Volatile organic compounds in paint, adhesives, and solvents cause respiratory harm and fire risk.
prevention_measures: organic vapor respirator for spray work
no ignition sources near solvent-based products

ppe_types.name: Hard Hat
ppe_types.required_when: Any risk of objects falling from above

ppe_types.name: High Visibility Safety Vest
ppe_types.required_when: Any vehicles or equipment operating on site

ppe_types.name: Fall Protection Harness
ppe_types.required_when: Work above 6 feet where fall protection cannot be provided by other means

ppe_types.name: Safety Glasses
ppe_types.required_when: Any risk of eye injury from flying debris, concrete, or chemicals

ppe_types.name: N95 Respirator
ppe_types.required_when: Drywall sanding, concrete cutting, dusty environments

ppe_types.name: Rubber/Chemical-Resistant Gloves
ppe_types.required_when: Contact with wet concrete, chemicals, solvents

ppe_types.name: Organic Vapor Half-Face Respirator
ppe_types.required_when: Spraying oil-based paints, adhesives, or solvents

worker_roles.name: General Contractor
worker_roles.description: Licensed contractor responsible for entire project. Holds the permit. Coordinates all subcontractors.

worker_roles.name: Site Foreman
worker_roles.description: Day-to-day site supervisor. Creates daily logs. Coordinates trades. First point of contact for problems.

worker_roles.name: Master Electrician
worker_roles.description: Licensed to pull electrical permits. Responsible for all electrical work quality.

worker_roles.name: Master Plumber
worker_roles.description: Licensed to pull plumbing permits. Responsible for all plumbing work quality.

worker_roles.name: Safety Officer / Competent Person
worker_roles.description: OSHA-designated 'competent person' who can identify and correct hazards. Required for excavation work, fall protection, and scaffold inspection.
worker_roles.osha_reference: OSHA definition of 'Competent Person' per 29 CFR 1926

inspection_types.name: Pre-Pour Foundation Inspection
inspection_types.description: Inspector verifies rebar placement, form dimensions, and soil bearing before concrete is placed.
inspection_types.timing: After rebar set, before any concrete pour
inspection_types.consequence_of_skip: Structural failure risk — foundation may not support building loads

inspection_types.name: Framing Inspection
inspection_types.description: Inspector verifies structural framing connections, headers, posts, and load path continuity.
inspection_types.timing: After all framing complete, before any rough-in trades cover structural members

inspection_types.name: Rough Electrical Inspection
inspection_types.description: Inspector verifies all wiring, panel, and box placement before drywall covers it.
inspection_types.timing: After all rough-in complete, before insulation or drywall

inspection_types.name: Rough Plumbing Inspection
inspection_types.description: Inspector witnesses pressure test and verifies all drain, vent, and supply piping.
inspection_types.timing: After pressure test passed, before insulation or drywall
what_is_checked: air test at 5 PSI for 15 minutes

inspection_types.name: Rough HVAC Inspection
inspection_types.description: Inspector verifies ductwork installation, equipment locations, and ventilation requirements.
inspection_types.timing: After all rough-in complete, before insulation or drywall

inspection_types.name: Insulation Inspection
inspection_types.description: Inspector verifies insulation R-values, coverage, and proper installation before drywall.

inspection_types.name: Final Inspection
inspection_types.description: Comprehensive inspection of all completed work. If passed, Certificate of Occupancy is issued.
inspection_types.outcome_if_passed: Certificate of Occupancy issued — building is legally occupiable

delay_types.name: Weather Delay
delay_types.description: Work stopped or reduced due to rain, high winds, extreme temperature, or other weather.
delay_types.contract_implication: Time extension typically granted, no damages

delay_types.name: Material Shortage
delay_types.description: Required materials not available — either not ordered in time or supply chain issue.
delay_types.prevention: Order materials with appropriate lead time. See lead_time_days in materials entities.

delay_types.name: Waiting for Inspection
delay_types.description: Work cannot proceed until inspector arrives. Inspector scheduling required 24-72 hours in advance.
delay_types.mitigation: Book inspections as soon as previous inspection passes — do not wait until ready to call

delay_types.name: Design Change
delay_types.description: Owner or architect changes design mid-construction requiring rework or pause.
delay_types.contract_implication: Usually results in change order for additional cost and time

delay_types.name: Labor Shortage
delay_types.description: Required workers or subcontractors unavailable on scheduled date.

delay_types.name: Equipment Breakdown
delay_types.description: Critical equipment fails mid-task requiring repair or replacement.
delay_types.prevention: Regular equipment maintenance, backup equipment plan for critical equipment

weather_conditions.name: Sunny
weather_conditions.impact_note: High temperatures can accelerate concrete curing and cause heat stress on roofing crews

weather_conditions.name: Rain
weather_conditions.impact_note: Light rain impacts outdoor work. Heavy rain stops concrete and roofing work entirely.

weather_conditions.name: Heavy Rain
weather_conditions.impact_note: Heavy rain stops concrete pours and roofing. Foundation excavations may flood.

weather_conditions.name: Thunderstorm
weather_conditions.impact_note: Lightning = stop all outdoor work. OSHA guidance: 30-30 rule (see lightning 30 miles away = 30 minutes until safe)

weather_conditions.name: High Winds (>25 mph)
weather_conditions.osha_reference: OSHA guidelines for wind restrictions vary by task
weather_conditions.impact_note: Cannot work on elevated framing or roofing in high winds. Trusses become sails.

weather_conditions.name: Freezing (Below 32°F / 0°C)
weather_conditions.impact_note: Concrete poured below 40°F requires cold-weather protection or admixtures. Exterior paint must not be applied below 50°F.

relationships.subject: stage_foundation
relationships.notes: Site must be ready before excavation

relationships.subject: stage_roofing
relationships.notes: No deck to attach roofing to without framing

relationships.subject: stage_electrical_rough_in
relationships.notes: Wire must be routed through framing cavities

relationships.subject: stage_hvac_rough_in
relationships.notes: Duct runs require framing to exist

relationships.subject: stage_plumbing_rough_in
relationships.notes: Pipes must be routed through framing

relationships.subject: stage_drywall
relationships.notes: Must cover insulation only after inspection

relationships.subject: stage_painting
relationships.notes: Surfaces to paint do not exist before drywall

relationships.subject: stage_framing
relationships.notes: Workers at height during wall and truss setting

relationships.subject: stage_framing
relationships.notes: Framing nail guns in constant use

relationships.subject: stage_hvac_rough_in
relationships.notes: Raw duct material has razor-sharp edges

relationships.subject: weather_high_winds
relationships.notes: High winds stop elevated framing work

relationships.subject: trade_electrician
relationships.notes: Master electrician license required to pull permits

relationships.subject: delay_material_shortage
relationships.notes: Material delays push back completion of stage which pushes back inspection scheduling
